name: 2026 Bayview Mackinac Race
organizing_authority: Bayview Yacht Club Mackinac Race Authority (OA), affiliated with US Sailing
start_area: Lower Lake Huron, off Port Huron, MI

start.ref: SI 6 / SI 8.2 — RC Signal Boat position
start.note: SI 6: starting area on the WEST side of the shipping channel, ~4.5 nm north of the head of the St. Clair River. RC Signal Boat anchored at approx N43°03.975' W82°25.050' (43.06625, -82.41750) = starboard end of the line; pin boat at the port end (SI 8.2). Single point stands in for the LINE (SI: coords approximate, no redress for minor deviation). First-class warning 1120 EDT Sat 2026-07-18 (SI 8.5); class flags + start times come in an SI ADDENDUM — not yet posted, watch the Official Board.

marks.name: Cove Island Virtual Gate
marks.note: SI 7.1 (coords match the NOR exactly). Gate ~1 nm long; SW mark N45°20.00' W081°51.00' left to PORT, NE mark N45°20.28' W081°49.63' left to STARBOARD; SW mark bears ~263° from the NE mark. Sail through the gate from a SOUTHERLY to a NORTHERLY direction (SI 7.1). WGS84; coords approximate per SI, no redress for minor deviation. At passage: photo of the boat's clock + primary nav GPS, and fill out + sign the Virtual Cove Island Gate card.

marks.name: Duck Islands
marks.note: NOR §11.1: leave to starboard on the approach to the finish. Geocoded (Nominatim, Duck Islands group SE of Manitoulin) — APPROX center; verify the island extent against the chart. Used as an avoidance obstacle (radius approximate); rounding SIDE not yet enforced.

marks.name: Bois Blanc Island
marks.note: NOR §11.1: leave to port on the approach to the finish. Geocoded (Nominatim) — center of a ~10 nm-wide island; radius 5.5 nm covers its extent for avoidance. Verify against the chart; rounding SIDE not yet enforced.

finish.note: SI 11.1 (coords match the NOR). Virtual GPS line Round Island Lighthouse SE corner → point on Mackinac Island; Round Island Light bears 180° magnetic upon finishing; cross East to West; sail ≥10 boat lengths beyond. The YB tracker records position at high frequency and ITS DATA DETERMINES THE FINISH TIME (SI 11.1 [NP]); finish defined by the transponder crossing (SI 1.1d, changes RRS 86.3). GPS+clock photo required at finish.

courses.name: Shore Course
courses.note: SI 7.2 / NOR §11.2. 204 nm (SI 18.3c). Thunder Bay Island and Bois Blanc Island to port.

start.ref: SI 6 / SI 8.2 — RC Signal Boat position
start.note: SI 6: starting area on the WEST side of the shipping channel, ~4.5 nm north of the head of the St. Clair River. RC Signal Boat anchored at approx N43°03.975' W82°25.050' (43.06625, -82.41750) = starboard end of the line; pin boat at the port end (SI 8.2). Single point stands in for the LINE (SI: coords approximate, no redress for minor deviation). First-class warning 1120 EDT Sat 2026-07-18 (SI 8.5); class flags + start times come in an SI ADDENDUM — not yet posted, watch the Official Board.

marks.name: Thunder Bay Island
marks.note: NOR §11.2: leave to port. Geocoded (Nominatim, off Alpena, MI) — APPROX; verify against the chart. Avoidance obstacle; rounding SIDE not yet enforced.

marks.name: Bois Blanc Island
marks.note: NOR §11.2: leave to port. Geocoded (Nominatim) — center of a ~10 nm-wide island; radius 5.5 nm. Verify against the chart; rounding SIDE not yet enforced.

finish.note: SI 11.1. Same virtual GPS finish line as the Cove Island Course.

zones.name: Lake Huron shipping lanes / traffic separation
zones.note: Not specified in the NOR; commercial shipping lanes exist in Lake Huron — source from SI/chart and human review before the optimizer treats them as exclusion zones.

requirements.text: Electronic SER compliance certification completed before registration.
Each crew member has a safety harness + compatible tether (≤2 m, ≥20 kN).
Jacklines (≥20 kN) rigged to reach all points on deck.
Display navigation lights (primary; backup on a separate power source available) between sunset and sunrise.
Three (3) SOLAS red hand flares (or approved equivalent) aboard, in date.
Electronic means to record a man-overboard position within 10 seconds.
Fixed 25 W VHF with the boat's MMSI programmed + a watertight handheld VHF.
Non-electronic charts for the race area (incl. US 14860, 14864).
Octahedral radar reflector ≥11.5 in (or equivalent).
Emergency tiller able to be fitted to the rudder stock (wheel-steered boats).
≥ $500,000 boat liability insurance valid for the race window; certificate carried aboard.
Final crew list submitted; register at Bayview YC or Port Huron on the registration date.

Carry the OA position transponder attached to the AFT-MOST portion of the stern pulpit at all times while racing (no stern pulpit → aft-most lifeline stanchion; alternates need Race Technical Chair approval). Its data determines the finish time. Turn it in at the Skipper Check-In Desk within 8 h of finishing ($1,000 charge if never returned).

Display the backstay flag (in the Skipper's Packet) from the backstay ≥6 ft above deck — from before entering the Starting/Staging Area until ≥30 min AFTER starting, and for ≥30 min immediately BEFORE and AFTER finishing. No backstay → fly it from the stern pulpit.

At the Cove Island Gate (Div I/IV): photograph the boat's CLOCK + primary navigation GPS showing time and position, demonstrating passage between the gate marks (southerly→northerly). Also fill out + sign the Virtual Cove Island Gate card — turned in at the Skipper Check-In Desk.

Cross the finish line East→West (Round Island Light bears 180° magnetic); sail ≥10 boat lengths past the line. Then: fill out + sign the Start & Finish card, and call the Race Harbormaster on VHF 71 for the assigned docking zone. Check in at the Skipper Check-In Desk (Mission Point main lobby) within 8 h of finishing — turn in the card + the transponder.

At the finish: photograph the boat's CLOCK + primary navigation GPS clearly showing the lat/lon at the time of Finish. KEEP the photo for 30 days (scoring inquiries). The YB transponder determines the finish time; it must not be your primary nav GPS.
Report any crew or sail-number change after registration on VHF ch 72, monitored from 1000 on race day until the last start. Do NOT check in on VHF 71 (emergencies only).
No overboard discharge of toilets / holding tanks (Michigan & Ontario law).
Boats may be selected for SER inspection after finishing — notified on VHF 71 and directed to dockage; do not refuse or hinder inspection.
Leave the harbors by 1100 Wednesday after the race unless arranged (and paid) with the DNR.

Fill out + sign the Start & Finish card (in the Skipper's Packet) at the START and at the FINISH; a boat representative turns it in with the transponder at the Skipper Check-In Desk (Mission Point main lobby) within 8 h of finishing. Finish after 1000 Tue Jul 21 → email legible photos of the card to mackchair2026@byc.com + confirm by text 313-618-5339.

Monitor VHF ch 16 at all times while racing (SI 10.1). RC uses ch 71 (recalls, signals broadcast, harbormaster) — keep it clear except for emergencies; ch 72 = crew/sail-number changes + the 0800 race announcement.

If retiring for ANY reason: contact the PRO by voice/text at 214-244-4955 ASAP and notify the USCG on VHF 16. MOB, injury needing medical attention, or damage >$5,000 → submit the Withdrawal/Retirement/MOB/Accident form (Owner Login page) by 2026-08-08.

Emergency: USCG VHF 16. USCG Sector Detroit (833) 388-8724 covers Port Huron→Alpena; Sector Northern Great Lakes (906) 635-3217 covers Alpena→Mackinac; STA St. Ignace 906-643-6402. YB tracker red alert: lift the flap, hold the RED button 5 s, keep sky view until the green LED stops flashing. Distress comms with USCG take priority; inform the RC as soon as practicable after.

Protest/redress: email intent + nature to mackjury@byc.com within 1 h of finish or retirement (no email → text 313-402-4949); file online within 3 h of finishing (or by 1000 next day if finishing after 2100). Scoring inquiries: ali@byc.com within 3 h.

All crew aboard by 0800 Saturday 2026-07-18 in Port Huron (dock departure management). Race announcement on VHF 72 at 0800. AP flag ashore = postponed, first warning ≥2 h after it drops (monitor VHF 71 + the Official Board).
Class E gun 13:00 EDT (start #10 of 14; day's 1st warning 11:20). Class flag color PURPLE — confirm backstay flag flown per SI 8.6. B/A start together 13:30; Multihull last 13:40.

modifications.summary: Help in the form of information available to all boats is permitted even if only at cost; but 'at cost' help shall NOT include private forecast or tactical advice or information customized for a particular boat or group of boats while underway. (This is the basis of the three-tier architecture.)
trash definition excludes biodegradable sail ties used while setting sails
a boat finishes when its position transponder crosses the finish line from the course side; GPS photo required
ORC governs monohull racing; +1 spinnaker allowance with conditions; storm/heavy-weather sails excluded from the sail count
Great Lakes Multihull Racing Association rules for multihulls
apply to Cruising Division entrants (Division III)
Rules for Racing Around Waypoints apply (gate/waypoint passing, electronic position fixing — primary nav system precedence)
[NP] breaches are not grounds for a protest by a boat
changed re: protests by boats not involved in / did not see the incident

options: BYC Mack Cove custom wind mix (Cove Island Course)
BYC Mack Shore custom wind mix (Shore Course)
scoring.decided: RC decides/announces BayMac custom wind mix vs ORC WRS no later than the 0810 Weather Briefing on race morning; if WRS, the TCF is published ≥3 h before the start (SI 17.1).

provenance.si_status: 2026 SIs v5 (incl. Amendment #1, posted 2026-07-14 1800) INGESTED 2026-07-15: start position (SI 6, RC boat N43°03.975 W82°25.050), first warning 1120 EDT, distances 259/204 nm, finish + gate coords confirmed identical to the NOR, YB-tracker-determined finish, checklist items updated ('display-numbers' REMOVED — no such SI requirement; backstay-flag windows + Start&Finish/Gate cards added). STILL PENDING: the SI ADDENDUM with class backstay flags + class start times (SI 8.6) — watch the Official Board; SI changes possible until 0900 race day (SI 3).
provenance.review_status: NOR+SER extraction reviewed against SI v5 on 2026-07-15. Island marks (Duck/Bois Blanc/Thunder Bay) remain geocoded-approximate avoidance obstacles — unchanged by the SI (it names them without coords). Requirements now cover the SI procedural set; the full ~40-item SER equipment list is still a representative subset. NEEDS the user's sign-off before the Jul-17 playbook synthesis freeze.
provenance.extracted_by: Lab-0 manual extraction 2026-06-17; SI v5 re-ingest 2026-07-15
provenance.division_starts: SI Addendum #1 posted 2026-07-16 1330 (bycmack.com/si-addendum-1); ratings refreshed from ScratchPrintClass.cfm + ORC WPub 2026-07-17

tracker.note: VERIFIED 2026-06-28: bycmack.com/tracking is YB Tracking (yb.tl). Positions come from https://cf.yb.tl/API3/Race/bayviewmack2026/GetPositions?t=0 — JSON, teams[].name + latest positions[] {latitude,longitude,sogKnots,cog,gpsAtMillis,dtfNm}. Confirmed against the real bayviewmack2025 feed (race-id convention bayviewmack<year>). The user confirmed the tracker is ALLOWED + normal in-race for Bayview Mackinac (rules_profile.tracker_permitted=true). The 2026 id returns {error:...} (not published yet) → dormant until ~July 2026; provider degrades to no positions. Use provider 'sample' on the bench.
